title: senecio atropurpureus subsp. tomentosus
intro: Senecio atropurpureus subsp. tomentosus, also known as Purple Groundsel, is a plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its purple flowers and downy, hairy foliage. Traditionally, Purple Groundsel has been used for its medicinal properties, which include the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, sesquiterpene alcohols, and other compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Purple Groundsel can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
